items.title: MMC Bans Doctor Fee-Splitting By Hospitals, Insurers, TPAs
items.summary: MMC has banned fee-splitting practices involving specialists or GPs by arrangements between private hospitals and ITOs or TPAs, amid payers squeezing providers for discounts that force doctors to cut their professional fees to stay on insurer/TPA panels. The post MMC Bans Doctor Fee-Splitting By Hospitals, Insurers, TPAs appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Tdap Immunisation For Pregnant Mums Prevents Deadly ‘100-Day Cough’ In Young Babies
items.summary: Tdap vaccine has been proven to be safe for both mother and baby, and is widely used around the world, showing no increased risk to pregnancies. The post Tdap Immunisation For Pregnant Mums Prevents Deadly ‘100-Day Cough’ In Young Babies appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Multilingual Clinic Appointment Cards Ensure Continuity Of Care, Says Contributor
items.summary: A contributor who was directly involved in the National Health Literacy Policy drafted by MOH and WHO defends the use of Chinese-language clinic appointment cards to ensure continuity of care. Diseases don't recognise race, religion or political ideology. The post Multilingual Clinic Appointment Cards Ensure Continuity Of Care, Says Contributor appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia’s Blind Spot On Fake Medicines: Why Ancient Deception Still Thrives — Saiman Raj Aruldas
items.summary: Fake medicines have survived for centuries because human vulnerability has remained constant. Malaysia’s true challenge is not simply defeating counterfeiters, but overcoming the ancient habits that allow them to thrive. The post Malaysia’s Blind Spot On Fake Medicines: Why Ancient Deception Still Thrives — Saiman Raj Aruldas appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Clarify If Nicotine Vapes Now Illegal After Court Ruling: MMA
items.summary: MMA wants the Health and Finance Ministries to clarify if liquid nicotine remains on the Poisons List, after the High Court struck down its 2023 removal from the list, and if nicotine vapes are now effectively illegal and hence cannot be taxed. The post Clarify If Nicotine Vapes Now Illegal After Court Ruling: MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Don’t Appeal Nicotine Ruling, Raise Tobacco Tax Instead
items.summary: Instead of appealing the High Court ruling or attempting once again to remove liquid nicotine from the Poisons List, the government should raise cigarette excise duties to at least 77 sen per stick. This can generate an extra RM772m in annual tax revenue. The post Don’t Appeal Nicotine Ruling, Raise Tobacco Tax Instead appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Sabah Strengthens HIV Response Through Strategic Partnerships, Expanded Community Support
items.summary: The Sabah state government remains committed to ensuring that critical HIV/AIDS services reach rural, interior, and underserved communities across the state. The post Sabah Strengthens HIV Response Through Strategic Partnerships, Expanded Community Support appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Never Sacrifice Public Health For Revenue — Malaysian Pharmacists Society
items.summary: The Court’s findings are also an important reminder that process matters. Expert advice matters. Consultation cannot become a procedural exercise after decisions have effectively already been made. The Poisons Board is not a decorative committee. The post Never Sacrifice Public Health For Revenue — Malaysian Pharmacists Society appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Analysis: Court Decision Marks De Facto Ban On Nicotine Vape
items.summary: The court ruling means that liquid nicotine stays in the Poisons List because it was never (legally) removed in the first place. Hence there's now a de facto ban on nicotine vape: sales and tax collection became illegal instantly upon the court's decision. The post Analysis: Court Decision Marks De Facto Ban On Nicotine Vape appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Authorities Must Take Immediate Action After Court Ruling On Liquid Nicotine — MCTC
items.summary: MCTC hopes that this court decision will serve as an important reminder that all public health policies must be formulated transparently, with integrity, free from industry influence, and in line with Malaysia’s obligations under WHO FCTC and the UN CRC. The post Authorities Must Take Immediate Action After Court Ruling On Liquid Nicotine — MCTC appeared first on CodeBlue .
